title: passiflora foetida var. hispida
intro: Passiflora foetida var. hispida, also known as the foetid passionflower or wild passionflower, is a plant species in the Passifloraceae family native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The medicinal properties of Passiflora foetida var. hispida lie in its roots and leaves, which contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Passiflora foetida var. hispida include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
